species.physical_description: Large Asian elephant with smaller ears than African elephants and a rounded back.
fun_facts: Asian elephants can swim and use their trunk as a snorkel.
Elephants communicate using low-frequency sounds.
Matriarchs pass migration memory across generations.

species.physical_description: Medium-sized brown bird with black head, yellow bill, and yellow eye patch.
fun_facts: Common mynas are excellent vocal mimics.
They are highly adaptable to human-dominated landscapes.
In some regions they are managed as an invasive species.

species.physical_description: Domestic equid with long ears, compact build, and high endurance.
fun_facts: Donkeys have strong memory and spatial awareness.
They are more drought-tolerant than horses.
Their bray can carry over long distances.

species.physical_description: Large, fast-running ungulate with long legs and a flowing mane.
fun_facts: Horses can sleep standing up.
They have wide-angle vision due to side-placed eyes.
Foals can stand and move soon after birth.

habitat.primary: Urban and coastal settlements near humans
regions: Introduced in Africa and Middle East

species.physical_description: Grey-necked crow with glossy black wings and strong urban adaptability.
fun_facts: House crows are known for high intelligence and problem solving.
They often form large communal roosts.
They have expanded globally via human transport routes.

habitat.primary: Deciduous forests and urban tree cover

species.physical_description: Grey-brown hornbill with a long curved bill and prominent casque.
fun_facts: Hornbills are important seed dispersers.
Pairs often show long-term bonds.
Females nest inside tree cavities sealed with mud.

habitat.primary: Terai Arc Landscape — Western Nepal & Northern India

species.diet: Carnivore — apex predator. Primary prey: Chital deer, Sambar deer, Gaur, Wild boar, Nilgai. Opportunistic: Langur monkeys, porcupines, fish, birds.
species.physical_description: Large striped big cat with strong territorial behavior and high ecological importance.
fun_facts: A tiger's roar can be heard 3 kilometers away. The infrasonic (below human hearing) component of a roar may cause temporary paralysis in prey animals — stunning them before the attack.
Tigers are the only big cats with fully striped fur — no two tigers have identical stripe patterns, just like human fingerprints.
A Bengal tiger can eat 40kg of meat in a single sitting — equivalent to eating 160 hamburgers at once.
The Sundarbans tigers are the world's only sea-swimming tigers. They regularly cross 3km+ of open water between mangrove islands.
A tigress teaches her cubs to hunt over a period of 18 months — one of the longest parental education periods of any land mammal.
Tigers have antiseptic saliva — they lick their wounds and are documented recovering from serious injuries that would kill most animals.
A tiger can leap 10 meters horizontally and 5 meters vertically — the equivalent of jumping onto a second-story balcony.
The white spots on the back of a tiger's ears (ocelli) are thought to function as false eyes when the tiger is facing away — deterring attack from behind.
India's tiger population has grown from approximately 1,411 (2006) to 3,167 (2022) — more than doubling in 16 years, one of conservation's greatest success stories.
Tigers are the national animal of India, Bangladesh, Malaysia, and South Korea.

population_estimate.source_note: India figures from NTCA All India Tiger Estimation 2022. Nepal from Department of National Parks and Wildlife Conservation 2022 survey.

species.physical_description: Striped equid with herd behavior and high mobility across grassland ecosystems.
fun_facts: Each zebra has a unique stripe pattern.
Zebra herds can travel long distances with seasonal rain cycles.
They often coordinate vigilance within groups.
